title: Sinosideroxylon racemosum
taxonomy: Sinosideroxylon racemosum is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This family consists of approximately 200 genera and over 2,000 species. Sinosideroxylon racemosum is also classified as being within the genus Sinosideroxylon, which is a small genus of flowering plants native to China. Further classification of Sinosideroxylon racemosum places it within the subfamily Annonoideae. The exact classification of Sinosideroxylon racemosum can be broken down into the kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Magnoliophyta, clade Magnoliidae, order Magnoliales, family Annonaceae, genus Sinosideroxylon, and species S. racemosum.

parts: Sinosideroxylon racemosum, also known as the white ivorywood, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia.
